1.title: A circular letter
1.text: Many scholars believe Ephesians was not addressed to Ephesus alone but circulated among several churches in Asia Minor, which explains its broad, general tone.

1.question: With what kind of blessings does Paul say God has blessed us in Christ?
options: Blessings of the law of Moses

1.template: According as he hath chosen us in him before the {0} of the world, that we should be holy and without blame before him in love:

pairs.right: Becoming children of God by Jesus Christ
Ransom through the blood of Christ
God's will made known
Gathering all things together in Christ

1.template: in whom also after that ye believed, ye were {0} with that holy Spirit of promise,

1.question: What did Paul pray that the saints would receive from God?
options: The spirit of wisdom and revelation

1.question: According to verse 20, what did God work in Christ?
options: Raised him from the dead and set him at his own right hand

1.title: The church as Christ's body
1.text: Paul introduces a central image of Ephesians: Christ is the head and the church is his body, an idea he develops throughout the whole letter.

2.title: The prince of the power of the air
2.text: The phrase 'the prince of the power of the air' refers to Satan, portrayed as the one who works in 'the children of disobedience.' Paul contrasts this dominion with the new life believers receive in Christ.

2.question: According to verses 4-7, why did God quicken us together with Christ?
options: Because he is rich in mercy and for his great love
Because we kept the law of commandments
Because we were by nature children of the circumcision

2.template: For by {0} are ye saved through faith; and that not of yourselves: it is the gift of God:

pairs.right: Made alive those who were dead in sins
The gift of God by which we are saved
Created in Christ unto good works
Those who once were far off from Christ

2.template: For he is our {0}, who hath made both one, and hath broken down the middle wall of partition between us;

2.question: According to verses 19-20, upon what foundation are the saints built, with Christ as the chief cornerstone?
options: Upon the circumcision of the flesh
Upon the course of this world

3.title: Paul the prisoner
3.text: Paul calls himself "the prisoner of Jesus Christ." Tradition places the writing of Ephesians during his imprisonment in Rome, around A.D. 60–62.

3.template: Which in other ages was not made known unto the sons of men, as it is now revealed unto his holy apostles and prophets by the {0};

3.question: According to verse 6, what is the "mystery" now revealed concerning the Gentiles?
options: That they should be fellowheirs and of the same body in Christ
That they will be judged separately
That they will receive a new law

3.template: Unto me, who am less than the least of all saints, is this grace given, that I should preach among the Gentiles the unsearchable riches of {0};

3.question: According to verses 11–12, through what do we have boldness and access with confidence?
options: By the works of the law

pairs.right: Fellowheirs and of the same body
Minister by the gift of grace
Hid in God from the beginning

pairs.left: Whole family in heaven and earth
pairs.right: Named of the Father

3.title: Breadth, length, depth, and height
3.text: Paul uses four dimensions — breadth, length, depth, and height — to describe the love of Christ, which he says passes knowledge.

3.template: And to know the love of Christ, which passeth knowledge, that ye might be filled with all the fulness of {0}.

3.question: How does Paul close the chapter in verses 20–21?
options: With a warning to the Gentiles
With a doxology of glory to God in the church

4.title: The prisoner of the Lord
4.text: Paul calls himself "the prisoner of the Lord" because he wrote Ephesians during his imprisonment in Rome, about A.D. 60–62. He saw his imprisonment as part of his service to Christ, not as a cause for shame.

4.question: According to verse 6, how is the "one God and Father of all" described?
options: Who is above all, and through all, and in you all
Who dwells only in the heavens
Who is known only to a few

4.template: When he ascended up on high, he led captivity captive, and gave {0} unto men.

pairs.right: Special witnesses of Christ
Receive and declare God's word
Proclaim the good news
Care for and instruct the flock

4.question: For what purpose were the apostles, prophets, and teachers given, according to verse 12?
options: For the perfecting of the saints and the edifying of the body of Christ

4.template: That we henceforth be no more children, tossed to and fro, and carried about with every wind of {0}

4.question: According to verses 17–19, how do the Gentiles alienated from God live?
options: They give themselves over to lasciviousness, working all uncleanness
Keeping the unity of the Spirit

4.title: The old man and the new man
4.text: The image of putting "off the old man" and putting "on the new man" compares conversion to changing clothes: laying aside the former way of life and clothing oneself in a new nature created after God.

4.template: And that ye put on the new man, which after God is created in righteousness and true {0}.
Let him that stole steal no more: but rather let him {0}, working with his hands the thing which is good, that he may have to give to him that needeth.

4.question: According to verse 29, what kind of communication should proceed out of the saints' mouth?
options: That which is good to the use of edifying and ministers grace to the hearers

4.template: And be ye kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God for Christ’s sake hath {0} you.

5.template: Be ye therefore {0} of God, as dear children;

5.title: A sweetsmelling savour
5.text: The phrase "a sweetsmelling savour" recalls Old Testament offerings whose smoke rose to heaven as a sign of something accepted by God; Paul applies this image to Christ's sacrifice for us.

5.question: According to verse 5, the covetous man is likened to whom?

5.template: For ye were sometimes darkness, but now are ye {0} in the Lord: walk as children of light:

pairs.right: Makes things manifest
Unfruitful works to be reproved
Its fruit is goodness, righteousness and truth

5.template: And be not drunk with wine, wherein is excess; but be filled with the {0};

5.question: In verse 23, the husband is compared to what in relation to the wife?

5.template: Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the {0}, and gave himself for it;

5.question: According to verse 31, a man leaves father and mother and is joined to his wife, and the two shall be what?

6.title: The first commandment with promise
6.text: Paul quotes the fifth commandment (honor thy father and mother) and calls it 'the first commandment with promise,' because it comes with the blessing of long life on the earth.

6.question: According to verse 4, how should fathers bring up their children?
options: In the nurture and admonition of the Lord
According to the traditions of men

6.template: Knowing that whatsoever good thing any man doeth, the same shall he receive of the Lord, whether he be bond or {0}.

6.question: Why should we put on the whole armour of God?
options: To be able to stand against the wiles of the devil

pairs.right: Righteousness
Faith
Salvation
Word of God

6.template: Above all, taking the shield of {0}, wherewith ye shall be able to quench all the fiery darts of the wicked.

6.question: How does Paul describe himself in verse 20?

6.title: Tychicus, the letter bearer
6.text: Tychicus, mentioned in verse 21, was the beloved brother Paul sent to bring news and comfort the saints; he likely also delivered this epistle.

6.template: Peace be to the brethren, and {0} with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
